buildings.name: Window Box
buildings.flavor: Three carrots in a trough. Humble. Ambitious.
buildings.upFlavor: The sun finds them every morning. They lean into it.

buildings.name: Garden Plot
buildings.flavor: A proper row of your own. The neighbors are watching.
buildings.upFlavor: Two spades deep. The taproots sing.

buildings.name: Allotment
buildings.flavor: Committee-approved carrot production. The bees came free.
buildings.upFlavor: Your motion passes 7–2. The parsnip man abstains, furiously.

buildings.name: Market Stall
buildings.flavor: Sells out by nine. The parsnip man is livid.
buildings.upFlavor: A laminated sign that says HEIRLOOM. Sales triple. Nothing else changed.

buildings.name: Greenhouse
buildings.flavor: Climate-controlled. The carrots believe it is always June.
buildings.upFlavor: A fine rain, always at the perfect moment. The carrots applaud quietly.

buildings.name: Rabbit Union
buildings.flavor: They stopped eating the crop once you offered dental.
buildings.upFlavor: The rabbits now guard the rows they once raided. Solidarity forever.

buildings.name: Carrot Combine
buildings.flavor: Harvests a field a minute. Slightly haunted.
buildings.upFlavor: Each carrot is held, briefly, on its way up. Reviews are positive.

buildings.name: Mycorrhizal Exchange
buildings.flavor: The fungal stock market. The Tomato rings the opening bell.
buildings.upFlavor: The Parsnip knew about the drought before the clouds did. No charges filed.

buildings.name: Orbital Polytunnel
buildings.flavor: In space, every direction is down. Taproots love it.
buildings.upFlavor: Roots humming in the mist. Ground control weeps gently.

buildings.name: Carrot Singularity
buildings.flavor: All carrots, everywhere, all at once.
buildings.upFlavor: It blooms. Continents pollinate. Somewhere, a fair judge faints.

clickUpgrades.name: Calloused Thumb
clickUpgrades.flavor: The blister becomes a callus. The callus becomes technique.

clickUpgrades.name: Two-Handed Pull
clickUpgrades.flavor: One hand pulls, the other steadies the earth. Teamwork.

clickUpgrades.name: Grandma’s Trowel
clickUpgrades.flavor: It remembers every harvest it has ever made. Clicks gain +1% of your CpS.

clickUpgrades.name: Proper Technique
clickUpgrades.flavor: Grip the shoulders, never the greens. Everyone knows this. Nobody does it.

clickUpgrades.name: Green Thumbs (Both)
clickUpgrades.flavor: Medically inexplicable. Horticulturally undeniable. Clicks gain +2% of your CpS.

globalUpgrades.name: Companion Planting
globalUpgrades.flavor: Marigolds at the borders. Everyone grows braver together. +10% everything.

globalUpgrades.name: Heirloom Genes
globalUpgrades.flavor: Purple Dragon. Parisian Round. The old names still carry weight. +15% everything.

globalUpgrades.name: Free-Range Carrots
globalUpgrades.flavor: They roam. They return. Don’t ask how. +20% everything.

globalUpgrades.name: Second Spring
globalUpgrades.flavor: Winter files a formal complaint. It is not upheld. +25% everything.

synergyUpgrades.name: Sill-to-Stall Pipeline
synergyUpgrades.flavor: Every sill in town feeds your stalls. Window Boxes gain +5% per Market Stall.

synergyUpgrades.name: Crop Rotation
synergyUpgrades.flavor: The plots rest; the greenhouse doesn’t. Garden Plots gain +5% per Greenhouse.

synergyUpgrades.name: Union Allotments
synergyUpgrades.flavor: The rabbits dig. The committee approves. Allotments gain +5% per Rabbit Union.

synergyUpgrades.name: Combine Cooperative
synergyUpgrades.flavor: Stalls sell what the combine pulls. Market Stalls gain +5% per Carrot Combine.

synergyUpgrades.name: Greenhouse Futures
synergyUpgrades.flavor: June, traded openly on the exchange. Greenhouses gain +5% per Mycorrhizal Exchange.

synergyUpgrades.name: Windowsill Wide Web
synergyUpgrades.flavor: Every window box, networked. Window Boxes gain +20% per Mycorrhizal Exchange.

synergyUpgrades.name: Orbital Union Charter
synergyUpgrades.flavor: Space rabbits. Unionized. Rabbit Unions gain +10% per Orbital Polytunnel.

ribbons.name: White Ribbon
ribbons.flavor: Third place at the village fête. It’s a start.

ribbons.name: Red Ribbon
ribbons.flavor: Second place. The judge lingered.

ribbons.name: Blue Ribbon
ribbons.flavor: First place. Somewhere, a gardener nods.

ribbons.name: Best in Show
ribbons.flavor: “In forty years of judging…” — you know the rest.

ribbons.name: Purple Rosette
ribbons.flavor: Awarded once a century, to a garden rather than a carrot.

ribbons.name: The Judge’s Tears
ribbons.flavor: There is no category for what you have grown. They give you the tears directly.

ribbons.name: County Sash
ribbons.flavor: Worn diagonally. Nobody explains the diagonal.

ribbons.name: Parish Perpetual Cup
ribbons.flavor: Engraved with every winner since 1811. All carrots now.

ribbons.name: The Golden Trowel
ribbons.flavor: Retired from competition. Awarded to you anyway.

ribbons.name: Regional Root Medal
ribbons.flavor: The region has been redrawn twice to keep you eligible.

ribbons.name: The Minister’s Ribbon
ribbons.flavor: Pinned by the Minister of Agriculture, hands trembling.

ribbons.name: Royal Warrant
ribbons.flavor: By appointment: purveyor of carrots to the Crown.

ribbons.name: The Empire Vegetable Prize
ribbons.flavor: The empire in question dissolved. The prize endures.

ribbons.name: Continental Grand Cordon
ribbons.flavor: Seven nations share one sash. It is yours.

ribbons.name: The Laureate’s Greens
ribbons.flavor: A wreath of carrot tops. Photosynthesis included.

ribbons.name: World’s Fair Grand Prix
ribbons.flavor: The fair built a pavilion just to hold the applause.

ribbons.name: The Blue Marble Rosette
ribbons.flavor: Visible from orbit, briefly, during the ceremony.

ribbons.name: Hemispheric Harvest Seal
ribbons.flavor: Both hemispheres. The paperwork took years.

ribbons.name: The Tomato’s Concession
ribbons.flavor: The Tomato rings the bell in your honor. No hard feelings.

ribbons.name: Planetary Soil Star
ribbons.flavor: The soil itself signed the citation. Do not ask how.

ribbons.name: The Parsnip Man’s Blessing
ribbons.flavor: He wept. He meant it. He grows carrots now.

ribbons.name: Lunar Exposition Medal
ribbons.flavor: Awarded in the Sea of Tranquility’s first allotment tent.

ribbons.name: The Tidal Lock Trophy
ribbons.flavor: The Moon agrees to always face your garden.

ribbons.name: Heliotrope Order
ribbons.flavor: The sun leans in for a closer look, every morning.

ribbons.name: The Perihelion Prize
ribbons.flavor: Awarded once per orbit, at closest approach. For the view.

ribbons.name: Asteroid Belt Cordon
ribbons.flavor: A ribbon of rocks, arranged into a bow.

ribbons.name: The Gas Giant Garland
ribbons.flavor: Jupiter’s spot is now slightly carrot-shaped.

ribbons.name: Kuiper Krown
ribbons.flavor: Spelling approved by committee, 7–2. The parsnip man abstained.

ribbons.name: The Heliopause Honor
ribbons.flavor: Where the solar wind ends, your rows continue.

ribbons.name: Interstellar Seed Sigil
ribbons.flavor: Etched on a golden record. Mostly carrot recipes.

ribbons.name: The Nebula Nosegay
ribbons.flavor: A bouquet of star-stuff, wilting over ten thousand years.

ribbons.name: Galactic Grange Fellowship
ribbons.flavor: The grange meets Thursdays, galaxy-time.

ribbons.name: The Spiral Arm Sash
ribbons.flavor: Worn diagonally across four hundred billion stars.

ribbons.name: Supercluster Cup
ribbons.flavor: Too heavy to lift. Displayed in situ.

ribbons.name: The Cosmic Web Weave
ribbons.flavor: Filaments of everything, braided like greens.

ribbons.name: Order of the First Furrow
ribbons.flavor: Dug before time. Harvested by you.

ribbons.name: The Heat Death Ribbon
ribbons.flavor: Awarded at the end of everything. Kept in advance.

ribbons.name: The Last Rosette
ribbons.flavor: There are no more categories. There is only the garden.

shed.name: Potting Bench
shed.flavor: Somewhere to stand, somewhere to plan. The whole world leans on it.

shed.name: Cold Frame
shed.flavor: A little glass roof against the frost. Spring arrives whenever you lift the lid.

shed.name: Heirloom Seed Library
shed.flavor: Every variety the world ever grew, filed under W for wonderful.

shed.name: The Grafting Table
shed.flavor: Two gardens, one stem. The judges have questions; the carrots have answers.

shed.name: Rainwater Cistern
shed.flavor: Every storm, banked. The garden drinks on schedule now.

shed.name: Bee Cooperative
shed.flavor: Unionized pollination. The rabbits sent organizers.

shed.name: Seed Vault
shed.flavor: Deep in the hillside, every spring that ever was, waiting.

shed.name: Orrery of Junes
shed.flavor: Brass rings, tiny suns. It is always the best week of June somewhere in it.

shed.name: The Perennial Engine
shed.flavor: It runs on continuity. Nobody remembers starting it.

shed.name: Propagation Bench
shed.flavor: One cutting becomes two. The mathematics of generosity.

shed.name: Mist House
shed.flavor: A building made of weather. Cuttings root out of sheer comfort.

shed.name: Tissue-Culture Lab
shed.flavor: A carrot in a jar, dreaming of being thousands.

shed.name: The Nursery Moon
shed.flavor: A second moon, but greenhouse. Rises only over the potting shed.

shed.name: Compost Heap
shed.flavor: Turn it again. Everything the garden was becomes what it will be.

shed.name: Sprinkler Network
shed.flavor: Every click lands a little wetter. Clicks gain +0.5% of CpS per valve.

shed.name: Parisian Round
shed.flavor: Round as a coin, sweet as a secret. The sill was always big enough.

shed.name: Amsterdam Forcing
shed.flavor: Forced? Encouraged, firmly.

shed.name: Nantes Half-Long
shed.flavor: The committee measured. Exactly half-long. Motion carried.

shed.name: Chantenay Red-Core
shed.flavor: The red core sells itself; the stall just holds it.

shed.name: Oxheart
shed.flavor: A carrot the size of a fist, a heart the size of June.

shed.name: Cosmic Purple
shed.flavor: The rabbits demanded purple. Nobody asked why. Solidarity.

shed.name: Autumn King
shed.flavor: Crowned each fall; harvested by appointment.

shed.name: St. Valery
shed.flavor: Old French stock. The fungi approve of tradition.

shed.name: Scarlet Keeper
shed.flavor: Keeps for months. In vacuum, forever.

shed.name: Lunar White
shed.flavor: Pale as moonlight. All carrots, eventually, are a little bit this one.

almanac.name: The First Seed
almanac.flavor: It weighed nothing. It changed everything.

almanac.name: A Pocketful
almanac.flavor: Ten futures, rattling softly.

almanac.name: The Seed Jar
almanac.flavor: Kept on the highest shelf, away from optimists.

almanac.name: The Seed Drawer
almanac.flavor: Labelled twice. Trusted once.

almanac.name: The Seed Chest
almanac.flavor: The hinges creak in springtime keys.

almanac.name: The Seed Cellar
almanac.flavor: Cool, dark, impossibly patient.

almanac.name: The Millionth Seed
almanac.flavor: Indistinguishable from the first. That is the point.

almanac.name: The Seed Silo
almanac.flavor: Visible from the next county. Audible in dreams.

almanac.name: A Nation of Seeds
almanac.flavor: They have not voted yet. They are waiting.

almanac.name: The Seed Billion
almanac.flavor: Astronomers ask to borrow the number.

almanac.name: The Seed Ocean
almanac.flavor: Tides of potential. Do not swim after eating.

almanac.name: Seeds Beyond Counting
almanac.flavor: The abacus retired. The seeds did not.

almanac.name: The Trillionth
almanac.flavor: A page that is mostly zeroes, and proud of every one.

almanac.name: The First Million
almanac.flavor: Pulled, weighed, recorded, eaten.

almanac.name: The First Billion
almanac.flavor: The scale asked for a holiday.

almanac.name: A Trillion Carrots
almanac.flavor: Laid end to end: a bad idea, gorgeous from orbit.

almanac.name: The Quadrillion Harvest
almanac.flavor: The word "bumper" files for early retirement.

almanac.name: Quintillion
almanac.flavor: The ledger switches to scientific notation and sighs.

almanac.name: Sextillion Summers
almanac.flavor: Each carrot got one perfect afternoon.

almanac.name: The Septillion Row
almanac.flavor: It bends with the planet now.

almanac.name: Octillion
almanac.flavor: Other gardens send pilgrims.

almanac.name: The Nonillion Garden
almanac.flavor: The soil remembers everything. So does this page.

almanac.name: The First Recorded Spring
almanac.flavor: Everything before this page is folklore.

almanac.name: Ten Springs
almanac.flavor: The rhythm settles in: bloom, seed, begin.

almanac.name: Fifty Springs
almanac.flavor: The Almanac develops opinions about frost.

almanac.name: The 250th Spring
almanac.flavor: Some pages are just the word "again", written warmly.

almanac.name: A Thousand Springs
almanac.flavor: The book grows a second spine.

almanac.name: Springs Uncountable
almanac.flavor: The margin notes have margin notes.

almanac.name: First Rabbit on Record
almanac.flavor: Golden, briefly, and gone. The ink still shimmers.

almanac.name: A Warren of Ten
almanac.flavor: They compare notes on being caught.

almanac.name: The Hundredth Guest
almanac.flavor: It signed the noticeboard on its way out.

almanac.name: A Thousand Visits
almanac.flavor: The hedge has a turnstile now.

almanac.name: Ten Thousand Golden Hours
almanac.flavor: The Almanac smells faintly of luck.

almanac.name: A Thousand Pulls
almanac.flavor: Blisters, then calluses, then legend.

almanac.name: Ten Thousand Pulls
almanac.flavor: The soil learned your handshake.

almanac.name: The Hundred-Thousandth
almanac.flavor: Somewhere, a glove union forms.

almanac.name: The Million-Pull Page
almanac.flavor: Hands, plural. Planet, singular.

almanac.name: Ten Million Tugs
almanac.flavor: The carrots started meeting you halfway.

almanac.name: The Great Heave
almanac.flavor: Recorded in every time zone at once.

almanac.name: A Billion Harvest Hands
almanac.flavor: The page is written in callus braille.

almanac.name: Ten Billion
almanac.flavor: The ground hums back now.

almanac.name: The Hundred-Billionth
almanac.flavor: Archaeologists will find thumbprints in the bedrock.

almanac.name: The Trillionth Pull
almanac.flavor: One planet, pulling together. Literally.

almanac.name: The First Million Planted
almanac.flavor: The shed doors never quite close anymore.

almanac.name: Fifty Million Furrows
almanac.flavor: The grounds crew requests a bigger wheelbarrow.

almanac.name: The Great Planting
almanac.flavor: A quarter-billion sprouts. The map needed reprinting.

almanac.name: A Billion Sprouts Deep
almanac.flavor: The Potting Shed is, legally, a region.

almanac.name: Sill City
almanac.flavor: Four hundred window boxes in one spring. The town is mostly trough.

almanac.name: The Quilted County
almanac.flavor: Four hundred plots, stitched with paths.

almanac.name: Committee of Committees
almanac.flavor: Three hundred allotments; the parsnip man abstains 300 times.

almanac.name: Market Saturation
almanac.flavor: Every corner a stall, every stall sold out by nine.

almanac.name: The Glass District
almanac.flavor: Two hundred fifty greenhouses. Sunset takes twenty minutes longer here.

almanac.name: General Assembly
almanac.flavor: Two hundred locals. One demand: carrots.

almanac.name: The Harvest Fleet
almanac.flavor: A hundred and fifty combines in formation. Tidy. Slightly haunted.

almanac.name: One Hundred Everythings
almanac.flavor: All carrots, everywhere, all at once — a hundred times over.

almanac.name: A Decent Toolshed
almanac.flavor: Fifteen upgrades on the wall, each with its hook.

almanac.name: The Full Rack
almanac.flavor: Thirty tools. The wall asked for reinforcement.

almanac.name: Every Tool There Is
almanac.flavor: The shed sighs, complete. Somewhere, a new tool is invented.

almanac.name: Ten Turnings
almanac.flavor: The heap steams gently, like a planet rehearsing.

almanac.name: The Fifty-Turn Heap
almanac.flavor: Fork technique now taught at the university.

almanac.name: Compost Centennial
almanac.flavor: One hundred turnings. The heap has alumni.

almanac.name: All Six Valves
almanac.flavor: The sprinklers play in key. The key is damp.

almanac.name: One of Each
almanac.flavor: Every strain, started. The catalogue clears its throat.

almanac.name: Five Generations Deep
almanac.flavor: The strains remember their grandmothers.

almanac.name: Ten Deep, All Rows
almanac.flavor: Pedigrees thicker than the soil.

almanac.name: Deep Roots Everywhere
almanac.flavor: Twenty-five generations, every variety. The fair judges retire, weeping.

almanac.name: The Lunar White, Perfected
almanac.flavor: Twenty-five generations of moonlight.

almanac.name: The Bench That Doubles
almanac.flavor: One cutting becomes two. The Almanac checks the math. It holds.

almanac.name: House of Weather
almanac.flavor: The mist house opens. Every sprout brings a friend.

almanac.name: The Jar That Dreams
almanac.flavor: Tissue culture: thousands of tiny gardens, politely queueing.

almanac.name: Nursery Moonrise
almanac.flavor: A second moon, and it waters things.

almanac.name: The Keystone Arch
almanac.flavor: Five stones, one shed, no gaps.

almanac.name: Fooled Once
almanac.flavor: Clank.

almanac.name: A Collection of Clanks
almanac.flavor: Twenty-five tin rabbits. The shelf grows heavy with shame.

almanac.name: Connoisseur of Decoys
almanac.flavor: One hundred. At this point it is on purpose.

almanac.name: The First Gamble
almanac.flavor: The stall appeared. Somebody dared, for all of us.

almanac.name: Fifty Market Mornings
almanac.flavor: The Parsnip Man respects you now. Somehow that is worse.

almanac.name: Rain on Record
almanac.flavor: The first recorded gentle rain. Everyone just stood in it.

seasons.name: Homestead Days
seasons.line: The garden at its own pace.

seasons.name: the County Fair
seasons.line: Bunting up, judges out, rabbits everywhere.
seasons.bonus: visitors call twice as often · +5% production

seasons.name: Market Days
seasons.line: Stalls at dawn, prices chalked before sunrise.

weather.name: Gentle Rain
weather.line: A fine rain, always at the perfect moment.
